original_abstract: This review explores the directed self-assembly of concave bis-monodentate ligands with square-planar metal cations to form discrete [M<sub>n</sub>L<sub>2n</sub>] coordination cages. We examine the structural principles governing the formation of these architectures, ranging from simple platonic topologies to more sophisticated molecular knots and interpenetrated dimeric frameworks.
humanized_abstract: Review of Directed Self-Assembly of Conical Bis-monodentate Ligands Leading to [M_n L_2n] Coordination CagesThis review examines the directed self-assembly of concave bis-monodentate ligands with square planar metal ions to form an enormous variety of discrete coordination cages ranging from simple platonic forms such as tetrahedra, pyramids, and various cuboid forms to more complex forms such as rings (e.g. molecular knots and pretzel analogues) and interpenetrated dimeric forms.

document.output: Review of Directed Self-Assembly of Conical Bis-monodentate Ligands Leading to [M_n L_2n] Coordination CagesThis review examines the directed self-assembly of concave bis-monodentate ligands with square planar metal ions to form an enormous variety of discrete coordination cages ranging from simple platonic forms such as tetrahedra, pyramids, and various cuboid forms to more complex forms such as rings (e.g. molecular knots and pretzel analogues) and interpenetrated dimeric forms.
